message:
fix(ft-autograd): propagate tensor read errors in backward_create_graph (frankentorch-aba)

backward_create_graph used unwrap_or_default() in two places when
reading per-leaf gradient buffers, silently turning any
DenseTensorError (UnsupportedLayout for non-contiguous results,
StorageSpanOverflow for malformed meta) into an empty Vec<f64>.
Two downstream effects:

- TensorBackwardReport reported an "empty but nominally success"
  gradient. Callers couldn't distinguish "leaf has no
  contribution" from "the read errored and we swallowed it".
- The persistent_grads accumulator zip-iterates over `vals`. With
  vals empty the zip loop body never fires, leaving the accumulator
  entry untouched — so on the second and later .backward() calls,
  any leaf that errored on read silently produced a no-op
  accumulation. Naive correctness tests (which check magnitude on
  the first pass) couldn't catch the regression.

This violated the project's autograd-correctness-outranks-kernel-
speed rule by hiding a real DenseTensorError as a missing gradient.

Replace both unwrap_or_default sites with map_err propagation:

- The leaves-extraction collect is rewritten as an explicit for
  loop so the `?` operator can short-circuit cleanly through the
  outer Result return type. Behavior for the OK path is identical
  — `requires_grad && reachable` leaves with a backing grad node
  produce Some(vals); everything else stays None.
- `.contiguous_values_as_f64().map_err(AutogradError::DenseTensor)?`
  surfaces the error at the backward boundary; AutogradError
  already has From<DenseTensorError>, so the integration is
  zero-impedance.
- Inline comments at both sites name frankentorch-aba and explain
  why we explicitly do NOT collapse to empty — a future reviewer
  who sees the ? and wants to "be defensive" needs to know the
  silent-empty path is the bug.

No new tests in this commit — the regression repro requires
constructing a non-contiguous gradient tensor through the public
autograd API, which is the work item recorded under
frankentorch-aba's acceptance criteria. The fix is a strict
subset of the existing API contract (errors that were always
possible now surface instead of being silently dropped), so all
existing autograd tests continue to pass; the issue body documents
the test that needs to be added next.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
